message:
fix(secret-scan): Skip detect-secrets when jq is missing (bd-kgg5)

Previously, when detect-secrets was installed but jq was not:
1. detect-secrets would run and find secrets
2. We couldn't parse the JSON output without jq
3. We'd silently fall back to regex patterns, losing findings
4. Tool was reported as "heuristic" hiding the actual issue

Now we check for jq availability before using detect-secrets,
ensuring we only use tools whose output we can actually parse.
Falls back cleanly to regex patterns when neither gitleaks
nor detect-secrets+jq are available.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-kbgh","title":"Implement ntm_kill_session() and ntm_interrupt_session()","description":"# Session Cleanup Functions

## Parent Epic: bd-9o2h (NTM Driver Integration Layer)

## Purpose
Cleanup sessions after processing or on error/interrupt.

## Implementation

```bash
ntm_kill_session() {
    local session="$1"
    ntm kill "$session" -f 2>/dev/null || true
}

ntm_interrupt_session() {
    local session="$1"
    ntm --robot-interrupt="$session" 2>/dev/null || true
}
```

## Kill Session
- Force kills the tmux session
- -f flag prevents confirmation prompt
- Idempotent (safe to call on non-existent session)
- Used after workflow completes or on fatal error

## Interrupt Session
- Sends Ctrl+C to agent panes
- Used to stop long-running agent work
- Can be followed by prompt retry

## Cleanup Pattern
```bash
cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions() {
    [[ "${AGENT_SWEEP_KEEP_SESSIONS:-false}" == "true" ]] && return 0
    
    local sessions
    sessions=$(ntm --robot-status 2>/dev/null | \
        grep -o '"name":"ru_sweep_[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
    for session in $sessions; do
        # Only kill sessions from this PID
        if [[ "$session" == *"_$$"* ]] || [[ "$session" == *"_$$_"* ]]; then
            ntm_kill_session "$session"
        fi
    done
}
```

## Session Preservation
Respects these flags:
- --keep-sessions: Never kill sessions
- --keep-sessions-on-fail: Keep failed repo sessions (default true)

## Trap Handler
```bash
trap 'cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions; save_agent_sweep_state "interrupted"' INT TERM
```

## Considerations
- Always capture pane output BEFORE killing session
- Capture artifacts for debugging failed repos
- Return true even on failure (cleanup should not fail sweep)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:50:13.532543977-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T19:05:33.945444761-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T19:05:33.945444761-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented ntm_kill_session(), ntm_interrupt_session(), and cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions() at lines 6246-6282."}
 {"id":"bd-kczb","title":"Implement cmd_agent_sweep() main function","description":"# Agent Sweep Main Command Function

## Parent Epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Purpose
Main entry point for `ru agent-sweep` command.

## Implementation Structure

```bash
cmd_agent_sweep() {
    local with_release=false
    local parallel=1
    local repos_filter=""
    local dry_run=false
    local resume=false
    local restart=false

    # Parse arguments (while loop with case)
    # ... --with-release, -j N, --repos=, --dry-run, etc.

    # ADDED: Concurrent instance lock (prevent multiple agent-sweep runs)
    local lock_dir="${AGENT_SWEEP_STATE_DIR}/instance.lock"
    if ! mkdir "$lock_dir" 2>/dev/null; then
        local existing_pid
        existing_pid=$(cat "$lock_dir/pid" 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
        log_error "Another agent-sweep is already running (PID: $existing_pid)"
        log_error "If stale, remove: $lock_dir"
        return 1
    fi
    echo $$ > "$lock_dir/pid"
    trap 'rmdir "$lock_dir" 2>/dev/null || true' EXIT

    # Check ntm availability (fail fast)
    ntm_check_available || { log_error "..."; return 3; }

    # Check tmux
    command -v tmux &>/dev/null || { log_error "..."; return 3; }

    # Load repos from config
    local repos=()
    load_all_repos repos

    # Filter to repos with uncommitted changes
    local dirty_repos=()
    for repo_spec in "${repos[@]}"; do
        local repo_path=$(repo_spec_to_path "$repo_spec")
        if [[ -d "$repo_path" ]] && has_uncommitted_changes "$repo_path"; then
            if [[ -z "$repos_filter" ]] || [[ "$repo_spec" == *"$repos_filter"* ]]; then
                dirty_repos+=("$repo_spec")
            fi
        fi
    done

    # Handle empty case
    if [[ ${#dirty_repos[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
        log_success "No repositories with uncommitted changes found."
        return 0
    fi

    # Dry run mode
    if [[ "$dry_run" == true ]]; then
        # List repos without processing
        return 0
    fi

    # Setup results tracking
    setup_agent_sweep_results

    # Handle resume/restart
    if [[ "$resume" == true ]] && load_agent_sweep_state; then
        filter_completed_repos dirty_repos
    elif [[ "$restart" == true ]]; then
        cleanup_agent_sweep_state
    fi

    # Setup trap handlers
    setup_agent_sweep_traps

    # Process (sequential or parallel)
    if [[ $parallel -gt 1 ]]; then
        run_parallel_agent_sweep dirty_repos "$parallel" "$with_release"
    else
        run_sequential_agent_sweep dirty_repos "$with_release"
    fi

    # Summary and cleanup
    print_agent_sweep_summary
    return $sweep_exit
}
```

## CLI Options to Parse
- --with-release: Enable Phase 3
- -j N, --parallel=N: Parallel workers
- --repos=PATTERN: Filter pattern
- --dry-run: Preview mode
- --resume / --restart: State recovery
- --phase{1,2,3}-timeout=N: Timeouts
- --execution-mode=MODE: plan|apply|agent
- --keep-sessions: Preserve sessions
- --keep-sessions-on-fail: Preserve failed sessions
- --attach-on-fail: Attach to first failed session for debugging
- --release-strategy=STR: Release policy
- --secret-scan=MODE: Security scanning
- --max-file-mb=N: Maximum file size limit
- --capture-lines=N: Lines to capture from pane output
- --json: JSON output mode
- --verbose / --quiet / --debug: Output control

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All CLI options parsed correctly
- [ ] Concurrent instance lock prevents multiple runs
- [ ] ntm and tmux availability checked before processing
- [ ] Repos loaded and filtered correctly
- [ ] Dry-run mode shows repos without processing
- [ ] Resume/restart state handled correctly
- [ ] Trap handlers set up for graceful shutdown
- [ ] Exit codes match ru standard (0-5)

 {"id":"bd-kips","title":"Fix installer: no releases (404) + cache bust downloads","description":"install.sh currently calls GitHub REST releases/latest which returns 404 ("Not Found") because the repo has no releases; this makes the one-liner curl|bash installer fail.

Fix:
- Detect 404/Not Found and fall back to installing ru from main (with clear warning) when no releases exist.
- Improve error messages for other API failures (rate limiting, etc.).
- Add cache-busting to download URLs (main/release asset fetches) to reduce stale caching.
- Update README/installer header examples.

Acceptance:
- One-liner install.sh works even when there are no releases.
- Curl caching issues are mitigated for downloaded artifacts.
- ShellCheck clean for changed scripts.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GreenBeacon","created_at":"2026-01-04T21:39:01.706193748-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T21:43:37.116875117-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T21:43:37.116875117-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 047d270:
- Detect 404 response from GitHub releases/latest API
- Fall back to main branch installation with clear warnings
- Add cache-busting query params to bypass CDN/proxy caches
- Improve error messages for rate limiting (403) and other API errors
- ShellCheck clean, tested manually"}
 {"id":"bd-koxf","title":"Define review plan artifact schema and validation","description":"# Task: Define Review Plan Artifact Schema and Validation

## Purpose
Define the machine-readable JSON schema that Claude must produce for each review session. This artifact is the contract between the Plan phase and Apply phase.

## Background: Why a Structured Artifact?
- Apply phase needs to know what to do
- Can't scrape agent transcripts reliably
- Enables resume after interruption
- Provides audit trail
- Enables metrics without parsing text

## File Location
```
{worktree}/.ru/review-plan.json
```

## Schema (Version 1)

```json
{
  "schema_version": 1,
  "run_id": "20250104-103000-12345",
  "repo": "owner/repo",
  "worktree_path": "/home/user/.local/state/ru/worktrees/.../owner_repo",
  
  "items": [
    {
      "type": "issue",
      "number": 42,
      "title": "Authentication fails on Windows",
      "priority": "high",
      "decision": "fix",
      "notes": "Root cause: path separator in auth.py:234",
      "risk_level": "low",
      "files_changed": ["src/auth.py"],
      "lines_changed": 5
    },
    {
      "type": "pr",
      "number": 15,
      "title": "Add Redis caching",
      "priority": "normal",
      "decision": "skip",
      "notes": "Out of scope - adds external dependency",
      "risk_level": "n/a"
    }
  ],
  
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "q1",
      "prompt": "Should I refactor all path handling or just fix this case?",
      "options": [
        {"label": "Quick fix", "description": "Fix only auth.py (5 lines)"},
        {"label": "Full refactor", "description": "Modernize all paths (45 lines)"},
        {"label": "Skip", "description": "Not a priority"}
      ],
      "recommended": "Quick fix",
      "answered": true,
      "answer": "Quick fix",
      "answered_at": "2025-01-04T10:35:00Z"
    }
  ],
  
  "git": {
    "branch": "ru/review/20250104-103000-12345/owner-repo",
    "base_ref": "main",
    "commits": [
      {
        "sha": "abc123def456",
        "subject": "Fix Windows path handling in auth.py",
        "files": ["src/auth.py"],
        "insertions": 3,
        "deletions": 2
      }
    ],
    "tests": {
      "ran": true,
      "ok": true,
      "command": "make test",
      "output_summary": "12 tests passed",
      "duration_seconds": 45
    }
  },
  
  "gh_actions": [
    {
      "op": "comment",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "body": "Fixed in commit abc123. The issue was path separators on Windows..."
    },
    {
      "op": "close",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "reason": "completed"
    },
    {
      "op": "label",
      "target": "issue#42",
      "labels": ["fixed-in-main"]
    },
    {
      "op": "comment",
      "target": "pr#15",
      "body": "Thank you for the suggestion. After review, this is out of scope..."
    }
  ],
  
  "metadata": {
    "started_at": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
    "completed_at": "2025-01-04T10:45:00Z",
    "duration_seconds": 900,
    "context_usage_percent": 45,
    "model": "claude-sonnet-4",
    "driver": "local"
  }
}
```

## Field Definitions

### Top Level
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| schema_version | int | yes | Always 1 for this version |
| run_id | string | yes | Unique review run identifier |
| repo | string | yes | owner/repo format |
| worktree_path | string | yes | Absolute path to worktree |
| items | array | yes | Work items reviewed |
| questions | array | no | Questions asked/answered |
| git | object | no | Git state (may be empty if no changes) |
| gh_actions | array | no | GitHub actions to perform in Apply |
| metadata | object | yes | Session metadata |

### Item Object
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| type | "issue"\|"pr" | yes | Work item type |
| number | int | yes | Issue/PR number |
| title | string | yes | Item title |
| priority | string | yes | high/normal/low |
| decision | string | yes | fix/skip/needs-info/closed |
| notes | string | no | Explanation of decision |
| risk_level | string | no | low/medium/high/n/a |
| files_changed | array | no | Files modified for this item |
| lines_changed | int | no | Net lines changed |

### Question Object
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| id | string | yes | Unique question ID |
| prompt | string | yes | Question text |
| options | array | no | Available options |
| recommended | string | no | Agent's recommendation |
| answered | bool | yes | Whether answered |
| answer | string | no | Selected answer (if answered) |
| answered_at | string | no | ISO timestamp |

### gh_action Object
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| op | string | yes | comment/close/label/merge |
| target | string | yes | issue#N or pr#N |
| body | string | no | Comment body (for comment op) |
| reason | string | no | Close reason (for close op) |
| labels | array | no | Labels to add (for label op) |

## Validation Function
```bash
validate_review_plan() {
    local plan_file="$1"
    
    # Must exist
    [[ ! -f "$plan_file" ]] && { echo "Plan file not found"; return 1; }
    
    # Must be valid JSON
    if ! jq empty "$plan_file" 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Invalid JSON"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Required top-level fields
    if ! jq -e '.schema_version and .repo and .items' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
        echo "Missing required fields: schema_version, repo, or items"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Schema version check
    local version
    version=$(jq -r '.schema_version' "$plan_file")
    if [[ "$version" != "1" ]]; then
        echo "Unsupported schema version: $version"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Items must have required fields
    if ! jq -e '.items | all(.type and .number and .decision)' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
        echo "Items missing required fields: type, number, or decision"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Validate decision values
    local invalid_decisions
    invalid_decisions=$(jq -r '.items[] | select(.decision | IN("fix","skip","needs-info","closed") | not) | .decision' "$plan_file")
    if [[ -n "$invalid_decisions" ]]; then
        echo "Invalid decision values: $invalid_decisions"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Validate gh_actions if present
    if jq -e '.gh_actions' "$plan_file" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if ! jq -e '.gh_actions | all(.op and .target)' "$plan_file" >/dev/null; then
            echo "gh_actions missing required fields: op or target"
            return 1
        fi
    fi
    
    echo "Valid"
    return 0
}
```

## Plan Summary
```bash
summarize_review_plan() {
    local plan_file="$1"
    
    jq -r '
        "Repository: \(.repo)",
        "Items reviewed: \(.items | length)",
        "  - Fixed: \([.items[] | select(.decision == "fix")] | length)",
        "  - Skipped: \([.items[] | select(.decision == "skip")] | length)",
        "  - Needs info: \([.items[] | select(.decision == "needs-info")] | length)",
        "Commits: \(.git.commits // [] | length)",
        "Tests: \(if .git.tests.ok then "PASS" else "FAIL/NOT RUN" end)",
        "gh_actions pending: \(.gh_actions // [] | length)"
    ' "$plan_file"
}
```

## Testing
- Validate correct plan passes
- Validate missing fields rejected
- Validate invalid decisions rejected
- Parse all field types correctly
- Handle optional fields gracefully

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Schema documented with all fields
- [ ] Validation function catches all errors
- [ ] Example plans provided for testing
- [ ] Summary function works
- [ ] Optional fields handled correctly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:37:01.268595285-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:42:10.057519193-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:42:10.057519193-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented validate_review_plan(), summarize_review_plan(), and get_review_plan_json_summary() functions in ru script. Added 14 unit tests in scripts/test_unit_review_plan.sh. All tests pass."}
 {"id":"bd-kvu5","title":"[EPIC] Error Handling & Recovery","description":"# Error Handling & Recovery

## Error Type Mapping

| Scenario | ntm Exit | ntm Error Code | ru Exit | ru Behavior |
|----------|----------|----------------|---------|-------------|
| ntm not installed | N/A | N/A | 3 | Log install command |
| tmux not installed | 2 | DEPENDENCY_MISSING | 3 | Log install advice |
| Session already exists | 1 | RESOURCE_BUSY | Skip | Kill existing, retry |
| Spawn timeout | 1 | TIMEOUT | Skip repo | Log, continue |
| Send failed | 1 | INTERNAL_ERROR | Skip repo | Log, cleanup, continue |
| Wait timeout | 1 | TIMEOUT | Skip repo | Log, cleanup, continue |
| Agent error detected | 3 | (state-based) | Skip repo | Log, cleanup, continue |
| Rate limit detected | 3 | (pattern match) | Pause | Global backoff, retry |
| Network error | 1 | (pattern match) | Skip repo | Log, continue |
| Interrupted (Ctrl+C) | N/A | N/A | 5 | Save state, cleanup |
| Preflight failed | N/A | N/A | Skip repo | Record reason, continue |
| Validation failed | N/A | N/A | Skip repo | Block commit, preserve plan |

## ntm Error Codes
| Error Code | Meaning | ru Exit Code |
|------------|---------|--------------|
| SESSION_NOT_FOUND | Session doesn't exist | 3 |
| PANE_NOT_FOUND | Pane index invalid | 3 |
| INVALID_FLAG | Bad CLI arguments | 4 |
| TIMEOUT | Wait exceeded timeout | 1 |
| INTERNAL_ERROR | Unexpected Go error | 3 |
| PERMISSION_DENIED | File/tmux permissions | 3 |
| RESOURCE_BUSY | Session locked | 1 |
| DEPENDENCY_MISSING | tmux not installed | 3 |
| NOT_IMPLEMENTED | Feature not ready | 4 |

## Rate Limit Recovery
- Detect via ntm activity (rate_limited:true) or pattern match
- Trigger global backoff (shared across all workers)
- Exponential backoff with jitter
- Max wait: 10 minutes

## Crash Recovery
- Detect via ntm wait exit code 3 or ERROR state
- Capture pane output before cleanup
- Option to preserve session for debugging

## Orphan Session Cleanup
cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions() kills all sessions matching pattern:
- ru_sweep_*_$$* or ru_sweep_*_$$_*
- Respects --keep-sessions flag

## Trap Handlers
trap 'cleanup_agent_sweep_sessions; save_agent_sweep_state "interrupted"' INT TERM

## Partial Success Handling
- Continue processing remaining repos on individual failures
- Aggregate results at end
- Exit code reflects worst case (0=all ok, 1=some failed, 2=conflicts)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:47:04.091975853-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T16:47:04.091975853-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-kvu5","depends_on_id":"bd-bx6s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T16:59:20.171369182-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-okbr","title":"Implement core utility functions for agent-sweep","description":"Implements core utility functions used across agent-sweep implementation.

## Parent Epic: bd-mkoc (Agent Sweep Command Implementation)

## Purpose
Provide shared utility functions that are used by multiple components of agent-sweep.

## Functions to Implement

### has_uncommitted_changes()
```bash
# Check if repo has uncommitted changes (staged or unstaged)
# Args: $1=repo_path
# Returns: 0 if dirty, 1 if clean
has_uncommitted_changes() {
    local repo_path="$1"

    # Check for any changes (staged, unstaged, or untracked)
    if [[ -n "$(git -C "$repo_path" status --porcelain 2>/dev/null)" ]]; then
        return 0  # Has changes
    fi
    return 1  # Clean
}
```

### repo_spec_to_path()
```bash
# Convert repo spec (owner/repo[@branch]) to local filesystem path
# Args: $1=repo_spec
# Returns: path via stdout
repo_spec_to_path() {
    local repo_spec="$1"

    # Strip optional @branch suffix
    local repo="${repo_spec%%@*}"

    # Handle owner/repo format
    local repo_name="${repo##*/}"

    # Use configured projects directory
    local projects_dir="${RU_PROJECTS_DIR:-$HOME/projects}"

    echo "${projects_dir}/${repo_name}"
}
```

### load_all_repos()
```bash
# Load all repos from config files into array
# Args: $1=array_name_ref
load_all_repos() {
    local -n repos_ref=$1
    repos_ref=()

    local config_dir="${RU_CONFIG_DIR:-$HOME/.config/ru}"
    local repos_d="${config_dir}/repos.d"

    # Load from each file in repos.d
    if [[ -d "$repos_d" ]]; then
        for file in "$repos_d"/*.txt; do
            [[ -f "$file" ]] || continue
            while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
                # Skip comments and empty lines
                [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# ]] && continue
                [[ -z "${line// }" ]] && continue
                repos_ref+=("$line")
            done < "$file"
        done
    fi
}
```

### sanitize_session_name()
```bash
# Sanitize repo name for use as tmux session name
# Args: $1=repo_name
sanitize_session_name() {
    local name="$1"
    # Replace non-alphanumeric chars with underscore
    echo "${name//[^a-zA-Z0-9_]/_}"
}
```

### strip_ansi()
```bash
# Strip ANSI escape codes from string
# Useful for parsing pane output
strip_ansi() {
    # Use sed to remove ANSI escape sequences
    sed 's/\x1b\[[0-9;]*[a-zA-Z]//g'
}
```

### get_file_size_mb()
```bash
# Get file size in MB (for display)
# Args: $1=file_path
get_file_size_mb() {
    local file="$1"
    local size_bytes
    size_bytes=$(stat -f%z "$file" 2>/dev/null || stat -c%s "$file" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    echo "scale=1; $size_bytes / 1048576" | bc
}
```

### array_contains()
```bash
# Check if array contains element
# Args: $1=array_name, $2=element
array_contains() {
    local -n arr=$1
    local elem="$2"
    for item in "${arr[@]}"; do
        [[ "$item" == "$elem" ]] && return 0
    done
    return 1
}
```

### get_repo_name()
```bash
# Extract repo name from path or spec
# Args: $1=path_or_spec
get_repo_name() {
    local input="$1"
    # Handle both /path/to/repo and owner/repo formats
    basename "${input%%@*}"
}
```

### ensure_directory()
```bash
# Create directory if it doesn't exist
# Args: $1=dir_path
ensure_directory() {
    local dir="$1"
    [[ -d "$dir" ]] || mkdir -p "$dir"
}
```

## Location
These functions should be defined early in the ru script, in a utilities section, so they're available to all agent-sweep functions.

## Integration Points
- Used by: cmd_agent_sweep() for loading repos
- Used by: run_sequential/parallel_sweep() for path resolution
- Used by: session naming for tmux
- Used by: plan extraction for ANSI stripping
- Used by: file size checking for validation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] has_uncommitted_changes() correctly detects all change types
- [ ] repo_spec_to_path() handles owner/repo and @branch formats
- [ ] load_all_repos() loads from all files in repos.d
- [ ] sanitize_session_name() produces valid tmux session names
- [ ] strip_ansi() removes all escape sequences
- [ ] All functions work on both Linux and macOS","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:37:49.376668698-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T19:09:10.236614618-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T19:09:10.236614618-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all 7 utility functions: has_uncommitted_changes(), repo_spec_to_path(), load_all_repos(), strip_ansi(), get_file_size_mb(), array_contains(), get_repo_name(). ShellCheck clean."}
-{"id":"bd-omo4","title":"Denylist: Support nested directory patterns (*/node_modules/*)","description":"# Nested Directory Pattern Matching Gap

## Problem
The `is_file_denied()` function does not match patterns when they appear in nested paths.

For example:
- `node_modules/pkg/a.js` → correctly denied
- `frontend/node_modules/pkg/a.js` → incorrectly allowed

## Current Behavior
The function checks:
1. Direct path match: `$file_path` matches `$pattern`
2. Basename match: `${file_path##*/}` matches `$pattern`
3. Directory prefix match: `$file_path` matches `$pattern/*`

None of these handle `*/pattern/*` style matching.

## Expected Behavior
Files in nested `node_modules`, `__pycache__`, `dist`, `build`, etc. directories should be denied regardless of nesting depth.

## Proposed Solution
Add recursive directory matching by checking if any path component matches the pattern:
```bash
# Check if any path component matches a denied directory
local IFS=/
local -a parts=($file_path)
for part in "${parts[@]}"; do
    for pattern in "${all_patterns[@]}"; do
        case "$part" in
            $pattern) return 0 ;;
        esac
    done
done
```

## Impact
Low/Medium - Most repositories don't have deeply nested dependency directories in their tracked files, but this is a gap in the security guardrail.

## Discovered By
Test suite for bd-6p3o identified this during comprehensive testing.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-06T22:46:06.959811063-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T22:46:06.959811063-05:00"}

 {"id":"bd-p2ip","title":"Implement review lock mechanism (flock-based)","description":"# Task: Implement Review Lock Mechanism

## Purpose
Prevent concurrent `ru review` runs which would cause chaos with overlapping worktrees, duplicate API calls, and conflicting state updates.

## Implementation Details

### Lock File Location
```bash
REVIEW_LOCK_FILE="$RU_STATE_DIR/review.lock"
```

### acquire_review_lock()
```bash
acquire_review_lock() {
    mkdir -p "$RU_STATE_DIR"
    
    # Open fd 200 for locking
    exec 200>"$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE"
    
    # Try non-blocking lock
    if ! flock -n 200; then
        # Check who holds it
        local holder_info=""
        if [[ -f "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info" ]]; then
            holder_info=$(cat "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info")
        fi
        log_error "Another review session is active"
        [[ -n "$holder_info" ]] && log_error "Started: $holder_info"
        log_info "Use 'ru review --status' to check, or wait for completion"
        return 1
    fi
    
    # Write lock info
    cat > "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info" << EOF
{
  "run_id": "$REVIEW_RUN_ID",
  "started_at": "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)",
  "pid": $$,
  "mode": "$mode"
}
EOF
    
    return 0
}
```

### release_review_lock()
```bash
release_review_lock() {
    # Remove info file
    rm -f "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info"
    
    # Release flock
    flock -u 200 2>/dev/null || true
}
```

### Trap Handler Integration
```bash
# In cmd_review(), set up trap
trap 'release_review_lock; exit 130' INT TERM

# Ensure cleanup on any exit
trap_cleanup_review() {
    release_review_lock
    cleanup_stale_worktrees
}
trap trap_cleanup_review EXIT
```

### Stale Lock Detection
```bash
check_stale_lock() {
    if [[ -f "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info" ]]; then
        local lock_pid
        lock_pid=$(jq -r '.pid' "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info" 2>/dev/null)
        
        # Check if process still exists
        if [[ -n "$lock_pid" ]] && ! kill -0 "$lock_pid" 2>/dev/null; then
            log_warn "Found stale lock from dead process $lock_pid"
            rm -f "$REVIEW_LOCK_FILE.info"
            return 0  # Lock is stale
        fi
    fi
    return 1  # Lock is valid or doesn't exist
}
```

## Why flock?
- Atomic lock acquisition
- Automatically released on process exit (even crash)
- No race conditions
- Works on Linux, macOS, BSD
- Already used successfully in parallel sync

## Edge Cases
- Process killed with SIGKILL: Lock released by kernel, but .info file remains
- Machine reboot: Lock file and .info persist but are stale
- Multiple users: Each user has own state dir, so no conflict

## Testing
- Verify lock prevents second ru review
- Verify lock released on normal exit
- Verify lock released on Ctrl+C
- Verify stale lock detected after kill -9
- Verify lock info shows useful details

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Only one ru review can run at a time
- [ ] Lock info shows run_id, start time, PID
- [ ] Lock released on all exit paths
- [ ] Stale locks from crashed processes handled","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:53.209434378-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:56:47.476050107-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:56:47.476050107-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented full flock-based review lock mechanism with JSON info file (run_id, started_at, pid, mode). Added check_stale_lock() for dead process detection. Proper trap handlers for INT/TERM/EXIT. All acceptance criteria met: single concurrent review, detailed lock info, cleanup on all exit paths, stale lock detection.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-p2ip","depends_on_id":"bd-mnu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:52.831071716-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
